Strategies vs. rules:
key concepts

Before diving into the Strategy Builder, it helps to understand the two core building blocks — Rules and Strategies — and how each page is organized. This page gives you a high-level orientation so you can navigate confidently.

Rules
  • Individual logical conditions that evaluate payment or organization data
  • Each rule produces an action: approved, declined, pending, etc.
  • Organized in two tabs: Payments and Organizations
  • Rules can belong to one or more strategies
  • Expand any row to preview rule conditions in read-only mode
Strategies
  • Collections of rules that together define a risk or compliance decision flow
  • Each strategy has up to three version modes: Draft, Shadow, and Live
  • All strategies — organization and payment — appear in one unified list
  • An Entity Type column tells you which type each strategy belongs to
  • Expand a strategy row to review its latest versions across all modes

The Strategies page

The Strategies page shows every strategy in a single scrollable list. Unlike the Rules page, there are currently no separate tabs for organization vs. payment strategies — everything is in one table, with an Entity Type column that identifies each strategy's type. By default the list is sorted with organization strategies first, followed by payment strategies.

Strategies page — all strategies in one list, sorted by entity type by default

Expanding a row shows the latest version for each mode — click a row to open it, or Compare to diff two versions

What you can do on the Strategies page

Create a strategy — click Add Strategy in the top-right. For a full walkthrough, see Touring the Strategies list.

Expand a strategy row — click ▶ to reveal the three version modes. From there you can click any version row to open the full strategy page, or click Compare to diff two versions side-by-side.

Sort — click the ⇅ icon on the Name column header to toggle ascending / descending order (also changes the organization/payment grouping).

Filter — click the filter icon on the Name column header to search for a specific strategy by name.

The Rules page

The Rules page is split into two tabs: Payments and Organizations. Each tab has its own table with rules for that entity type — this is the key structural difference from the Strategies page, which keeps everything in a single list.

Rules page — switch between Payments and Organizations tabs to view rules by entity type

Expanding a rule row reveals its full condition logic in read-only mode

What you can do on the Rules page

Switch tabs — use Payments and Organizations to view rules for each entity type separately.

Create a rule — click Add Rule in the top-right. For a detailed walkthrough, see Touring the Rules list.

Preview a rule's conditions — click ▶ to expand any row and see its logic in read-only mode.

Edit a rule — click the ✏️ pen icon to open the Rule Editor where you can modify the rule's logic, action, and metadata.

Find which strategies contain a rule — open the Rule Editor for that rule; it shows a list of all strategies the rule belongs to.

Key differences at a glance

Strategies pageRules page
Entity type separationSingle list with Entity Type columnTwo separate tabs (Payments / Organizations)
Row expansion revealsLast Live, Shadow & Draft versionsRule conditions (read-only)
Versioning✅ Live / Shadow / Draft modesNo versioning — rules edited directly
Default sort orderOrganization first, then PaymentBy rule ID (ascending)
Search / filterFilter + sort icons on Name columnSwitch tab to change entity type
